title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: BeerKan (Beerkan Estimation Soil Transfer parameters) protocol low-cost field method: fixed volume water poured repeatedly inside ring, time pour completely infiltrate recorded. Unlike standard ring Minidisk methods, measurement proceeds zero pressure head (ponded). BEST algorithm (Lassabatère et al., 2006) fits Haverkamp et al. (1994) quasi-exact implicit infiltration model resulting (t) series, recovering saturated hydraulic conductivity Ks sorptivity S. Van Genuchten scale parameter α recovered analytically.

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: BeerKan run pours fixed 100 mL water 10 times ring (radius 5 cm), recording long pour takes disappear. infiltration time increases early (soil initially dry) stabilises near 35 s soil approaches steady state.

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: beerkan_cumulative() accumulates time volume, converts depth using ring area.  curve transitions rapid early phase linear steady-state, consistent Haverkamp model.
code: cum <- beerkan_cumulative(run, volume_col = volume, time_col = time, radius = 5) cum |> select(pour, volume, time, .cumulative_time, .infiltration) #> # A tibble: 10 × 5 #>     pour volume  time .cumulative_time .infiltration #>    <int>  <dbl> <dbl>            <dbl>         <dbl> #>  1     1    100    12               12          1.27 #>  2     2    100    18               30          2.55 #>  3     3    100    25               55          3.82 #>  4     4    100    30               85          5.09 #>  5     5    100    33              118          6.37 #>  6     6    100    34              152          7.64 #>  7     7    100    35              187          8.91 #>  8     8    100    35              222         10.2  #>  9     9    100    36              258         11.5  #> 10    10    100    35              293         12.7 ggplot(cum, aes(x = .cumulative_time, y = .infiltration)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(title = "Cumulative BeerKan infiltration",        x = "Cumulative time (s)", y = "Infiltration depth (cm)")

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: BEST need Van Genuchten shape parameter n. Since experiment runs zero pressure head, use suction = 0 retrieve shape parameters (.NA, expected):
code: soil_meta <- tibble(texture = "loam", theta_s = 0.43, theta_i = 0.12) |>   infiltration_vg_params(texture = texture, suction = 0)  soil_meta |> select(texture, theta_s, theta_i, .n, .alpha, .A) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   texture theta_s theta_i    .n .alpha    .A #>   <chr>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 loam       0.43    0.12  1.56  0.036    NA

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: running BEST, quick Philip two-term fit gives preliminary sorptivity conductivity proxy. C₁ (0.0235 cm/s) rough Ks proxy; BEST refine using full Haverkamp model.
code: philip <- fit_infiltration(cum,                            infiltration_col = .infiltration,                            sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) philip #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>     .C2    .C1 .C2_std_error .C1_std_error .convergence #>   <dbl>  <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 0.343 0.0235        0.0298       0.00141 TRUE

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: fit_best() accepts cumulative (t) data together soil moisture parameters (theta_s, theta_i) VG shape exponent n. key outputs:
code: best <- fit_best(   cum,   infiltration_col = .infiltration,   time_col         = .cumulative_time,   theta_s          = 0.43,   theta_i          = 0.12,   n                = soil_meta$.n ) best #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>      .Ks    .S .alpha .Ks_std_error .S_std_error .steady_n .convergence #>    <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>     <int> <lgl>        #> 1 0.0360 0.261   1.24      0.000144      0.00215         4 TRUE

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: Three fitting strategies available. "steady" (default) uses last steady_n = 4 points define steady-state window; "slope" uses early-time transient estimate S independently.
code: methods <- c("steady", "slope", "intercept")  results <- lapply(methods, function(m) {   fit_best(     cum,     infiltration_col = .infiltration,     time_col         = .cumulative_time,     theta_s = 0.43, theta_i = 0.12,     n       = soil_meta$.n,     method  = m   ) |>     mutate(method = m) }) |>   bind_rows()  results |> select(method, .Ks, .S, .alpha, .convergence) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   method       .Ks    .S .alpha .convergence #>   <chr>      <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 steady    0.0360 0.261  1.24  TRUE         #> 2 slope     0.0360 0.569  0.261 TRUE         #> 3 intercept 0.0360 0.261  1.24  TRUE

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: field campaigns, group site process runs together. Site S2 (sandy loam) shows higher Ks, consistent coarser texture faster infiltration times.
code: # Per-site soil parameters are embedded as columns so fit_best() can resolve # them via tidy evaluation within each group. field_runs <- tibble(   site    = rep(c("S1", "S2"), each = 10),   pour    = rep(1:10, 2),   volume  = 100,   time    = c(     12, 18, 25, 30, 33, 34, 35, 35, 36, 35,   # S1 — loam      8, 11, 14, 17, 19, 20, 20, 21, 20, 21    # S2 — sandy loam (faster)   ),   theta_s = rep(c(0.43, 0.40), each = 10),   theta_i = rep(c(0.12, 0.08), each = 10),   n       = rep(c(1.56, 1.89), each = 10) )  multi_cum <- field_runs |>   group_by(site) |>   beerkan_cumulative(volume_col = volume, time_col = time, radius = 5)  multi_best <- multi_cum |>   group_by(site) |>   fit_best(     infiltration_col = .infiltration,     time_col         = .cumulative_time,     theta_s          = theta_s,     theta_i          = theta_i,     n                = n   )  multi_best |> select(site, .Ks, .S, .alpha, .convergence) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site     .Ks    .S .alpha .convergence #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 S1    0.0360 0.261   1.24 TRUE         #> 2 S2    0.0618 0.359   1.21 TRUE

title: BeerKan experiment and the BEST algorithm
text: Haverkamp, R., Ross, P. J., Smettem, K. R. J., & Parlange, J.-Y. (1994). Three-dimensional analysis infiltration disc infiltrometer: 2. Physically based infiltration equation. Water Resources Research, 30(11), 2931–2935. Lassabatère, L., Angulo-Jaramillo, R., Soria Ugalde, J. M., Cuenca, R., Braud, ., & Haverkamp, R. (2006). Beerkan estimation soil transfer parameters infiltration experiments—BEST. Soil Science Society America Journal, 70(2), 521–532. https://doi.org/10.2136/sssaj2005.0026

title: Minidisk tension-disc infiltrometer workflow
text: Minidisk Infiltrometer (Decagon Devices) delivers water controlled tension (suction) saturation. Zhang (1997) method extracts unsaturated hydraulic conductivity K(h) two-step analysis: Fit Philip (1957) two-term polynomial cumulative infiltration recover conductivity proxy C₁. Divide C₁ soil-specific shape parameter (derived Van Genuchten parameters applied tension) get K(h). minidisk_conductivity() wraps steps 2 single call, giving four-step pipeline raw field readings K(h): underlying functions infiltration_vg_params(), parameter_A_zhang(), hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk() remain exported cases need control.

title: Minidisk tension-disc infiltrometer workflow
text: typical Minidisk run records reservoir volume (mL) fixed time intervals. disc radius 2.25 cm (standard instrument). full pipeline raw readings K(h): K(h) ≈ 6.45^{-4} cm/s 2 cm tension sandy loam sample.
code: raw <- tibble(   time   = seq(0, 300, 30),   # seconds   volume = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67)  # mL ) result <- raw |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = "sandy loam", suction = 2)  result |> select(.C1, .n, .alpha, .A, .K_h) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>       .C1    .n .alpha    .A     .K_h #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 0.00252  1.89  0.075  3.91 0.000645

title: Minidisk tension-disc infiltrometer workflow
text: field campaigns multiple samples, group site infiltration_cumulative(). grouping now preserved cumulative calculations, one group_by() needed whole pipeline.
code: multi <- tibble(   site   = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 11),   time   = rep(seq(0, 300, 30), 2),   volume = c(     95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67,   # site A — sandy loam     95, 87, 81, 76, 72, 68, 65, 63, 61, 59, 58    # site B — loamy sand   ) )  # Per-site metadata (texture, suction) meta <- tibble(   site    = c("A", "B"),   texture = c("sandy loam", "loamy sand"),   suction = c(2, 2) ) multi_result <- multi |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   left_join(meta, by = "site") |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = texture, suction = suction)  multi_result |> select(site, texture, .C1, .A, .K_h) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site  texture        .C1    .A     .K_h #>   <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 A     sandy loam 0.00252  3.91 0.000645 #> 2 B     loamy sand 0.00137  2.43 0.000565

title: Minidisk tension-disc infiltrometer workflow
text: non-standard disc radii suction levels outside Decagon table, pass method = "zhang" compute analytically Zhang (1997) rather looking . radius argument used mode. analytical values differ slightly tabulated ones — approaches valid; choice depends whether suction radius match Decagon reference conditions.
code: multi |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   left_join(meta, by = "site") |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = texture, suction = suction,                         method = "zhang", radius = 2.25) |>   select(site, texture, .A, .K_h) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   site  texture       .A     .K_h #>   <chr> <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 A     sandy loam  3.82 0.000660 #> 2 B     loamy sand  4.21 0.000326

code: multi |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   ggplot(aes(x = time, y = .infiltration, colour = site)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(     title  = "Cumulative infiltration — Minidisk",     x      = "Time (s)",     y      = "Cumulative infiltration (cm)",     colour = "Site"   )

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: Standard ponded ring infiltrometers (single- double-ring) measure fast water infiltrates saturation. Three empirical models available: processing pipeline : infiltration_cumulative() — convert volume readings (t) infiltration_rate() — compute interval rates (Horton) Fit one models resulting series

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: 10 cm radius ring measured one hour, reading reservoir volume (mL) every six minutes.

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: first .rate row NA — preceding interval initial observation.
code: ring <- ring_raw |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) |>   infiltration_rate(time_col = time, infiltration_col = .infiltration)  ring |> select(time, .infiltration, .rate, .time_mid) #> # A tibble: 11 × 4 #>     time .infiltration     .rate .time_mid #>    <dbl>         <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1     0         0     NA               NA #>  2   360         0.175  0.000486       180 #>  3   720         0.296  0.000336       540 #>  4  1080         0.392  0.000265       900 #>  5  1440         0.477  0.000239      1260 #>  6  1800         0.554  0.000212      1620 #>  7  2160         0.630  0.000212      1980 #>  8  2520         0.703  0.000203      2340 #>  9  2880         0.777  0.000203      2700 #> 10  3240         0.847  0.000195      3060 #> 11  3600         0.920  0.000203      3420 ggplot(ring, aes(x = time, y = .infiltration)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(title = "Cumulative infiltration — ring infiltrometer",        x = "Time (s)", y = "Cumulative infiltration (cm)") ring |>   filter(!is.na(.rate)) |>   ggplot(aes(x = .time_mid, y = .rate)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(title = "Infiltration rate — ring infiltrometer",        x = "Time (s)", y = "Rate (cm/s)")

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: C₁ (1.29^{-4} cm/s) proxy Ksat ponded conditions; C₂ (0.00764 cm/s^0.5) sorptivity proxy.
code: philip <- fit_infiltration(ring,                            infiltration_col = .infiltration,                            sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) philip #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>       .C2      .C1 .C2_std_error .C1_std_error .convergence #>     <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 0.00764 0.000129      0.000336    0.00000509 TRUE

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: Horton (1940) model fits exponential decay infiltration rate series: f(t)=fc+(f0−fc)⋅e−ktf(t) = f_c + (f_0 - f_c) \cdot e^{-k t} fc ≈ Ksat. Horton estimates: fc = 2^{-4} cm/s ≈ Ksat, f0 = 6.11^{-4} cm/s (initial rate), k = 0.002025 s⁻¹ (decay constant). Visualise fitted curve:
code: horton <- fit_infiltration_horton(ring,                                   rate_col = .rate,                                   time_col = .time_mid) horton #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>        .fc     .f0      .k .fc_std_error .f0_std_error .k_std_error .convergence #>      <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 0.000200 6.11e-4 0.00202    0.00000209    0.00000947    0.0000757 TRUE # Predicted rate curve over the observed time range t_pred <- seq(180, 3420, 60) pred_horton <- tibble(   time = t_pred,   rate = horton$.fc + (horton$.f0 - horton$.fc) * exp(-horton$.k * t_pred) )  ring |>   filter(!is.na(.rate)) |>   ggplot(aes(x = .time_mid, y = .rate)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line(data = pred_horton, aes(x = time, y = rate),             colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_hline(yintercept = horton$.fc, linetype = "dashed", colour = "steelblue") +   labs(title    = "Horton model fit",        subtitle = paste0("fc = ", signif(horton$.fc, 3), " cm/s"),        x = "Time (s)", y = "Rate (cm/s)")

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: Kostiakov (1932) power model fits cumulative infiltration: (t)=⋅tb(>0,0<b<1)(t) = \cdot t^b \qquad (> 0,\ 0 < b < 1) Note Kostiakov model approach finite steady-state rate, provide Ksat estimate.
code: kostiakov <- fit_infiltration_kostiakov(ring,                                         infiltration_col = .infiltration,                                         time_col         = time) kostiakov #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>        .a    .b .a_std_error .b_std_error .convergence #>     <dbl> <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 0.00269 0.712     0.000120      0.00570 TRUE

title: Ponded ring infiltration workflow
text: Group raw data site fit three models single pipeline using group_by().
code: multi_ring <- tibble(   site   = rep(c("Field_1", "Field_2"), each = 11),   time   = rep(seq(0, 3600, 360), 2),   volume = c(     500, 445, 407, 377, 350, 326, 302, 279, 256, 234, 211,   # loam     500, 404, 349, 308, 271, 237, 202, 168, 134, 100,  66    # sandy loam   ) )  # Cumulative + rate in one pass multi_cum <- multi_ring |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) |>   infiltration_rate(time_col = time, infiltration_col = .infiltration)  # Philip fit multi_philip <- multi_cum |>   group_by(site) |>   fit_infiltration(infiltration_col = .infiltration,                    sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) multi_philip #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   site        .C2      .C1 .C2_std_error .C1_std_error .convergence #>   <chr>     <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 Field_1 0.00764 0.000129      0.000336    0.00000509 TRUE         #> 2 Field_2 0.0128  0.000166      0.000547    0.00000829 TRUE  # Horton fit multi_horton <- multi_cum |>   group_by(site) |>   fit_infiltration_horton(rate_col = .rate, time_col = .time_mid) multi_horton |> select(site, .fc, .f0, .k, .convergence) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site         .fc      .f0      .k .convergence #>   <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 Field_1 0.000200 0.000611 0.00202 TRUE         #> 2 Field_2 0.000301 0.00124  0.00301 TRUE

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
text: Tidy Tools Soil Infiltration Analysis Part TidySoils ecosystem. tidysoilinfiltration brings three field infiltration methods tidyverse: Minidisk tension-disc infiltrometer (Zhang 1997), standard ponded ring infiltration (Philip 1957; Horton 1940; Kostiakov 1932), BeerKan ring experiment analysed BEST algorithm (Lassabatère et al. 2006). Every function pipe-compatible, uses tidy evaluation column arguments, vectorised raster-scale workflows.

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Taakefyrsten/tidysoilinfiltration")

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
text: Alternatively, compute analytically VG parameters instead lookup table:
code: library(tidysoilinfiltration) library(dplyr)  raw_data <- tibble::tibble(   sample = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 11),   time   = rep(seq(0, 300, 30), 2),   volume = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67,              83, 77, 64, 61, 58, 45, 42, 35, 29, 17, 15) )  meta <- tibble::tibble(   sample  = c("A", "B"),   texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   suction = c(4, 2)          # applied tension in cm )  raw_data |>   group_by(sample) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>  # radius = 2.25 cm default   fit_infiltration(infiltration_col = .infiltration,                    sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) |>   left_join(meta, by = "sample") |>   infiltration_vg_params(texture = texture, suction = suction) |>   hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk(C1 = .C1, A = .A) # Adds .K_h (cm/s) — unsaturated hydraulic conductivity at the applied tension # After fit_infiltration() and obtaining n and alpha from another source: result |>   parameter_A_zhang(n = n_col, alpha = alpha_col, suction = suction) |>   hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk(C1 = .C1, A = .A)

code: ring_data |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) |>   fit_infiltration(infiltration_col = .infiltration,                    sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) # .C1 ≈ Ksat (cm/s)

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
code: ring_data |>   group_by(site) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) |>   fit_infiltration_kostiakov(infiltration_col = .infiltration, time_col = time) # Returns .a and .b; note: Kostiakov f → 0 as t → ∞ (no true Ksat)

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
text: BeerKan data different structure standard ring data: instead measuring volume fixed times, pour fixed volume record long takes infiltrate.
code: pour_data <- tibble::tibble(   site   = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 10),   volume = 100,    # mL per pour   time   = c(15, 22, 30, 38, 44, 48, 50, 51, 51, 52,   # seconds to infiltrate              25, 35, 45, 55, 62, 67, 70, 71, 72, 72) )  soil_meta <- tibble::tibble(   site    = c("A", "B"),   texture = c("loam", "sandy loam"),   theta_s = c(0.45, 0.40),   theta_i = c(0.12, 0.08) ) |>   infiltration_vg_params(texture = texture, suction = 0)   # suction = 0 for BeerKan  pour_data |>   group_by(site) |>   beerkan_cumulative(volume_col = volume, time_col = time, radius = 5) |>   left_join(soil_meta, by = "site") |>   fit_best(     infiltration_col = .infiltration,     time_col         = .cumulative_time,     theta_s          = theta_s,     theta_i          = theta_i,     n                = .n,            # VG shape parameter from texture lookup     method           = "steady"       # BEST-steady (default, recommended)   ) # Returns .Ks (cm/s), .S (cm/s^0.5), .alpha (1/cm) per site

title: tidysoilinfiltration — Tidy soil infiltration analysis
text: functions follow TidySoils conventions: Pipe-compatible — every function takes data frame first argument returns tibble result columns (prefixed .) appended. Tidy evaluation — column arguments accept bare column names, scalars, mix. Vectorised backends — R-level loops rows. Group-aware operations use dplyr::mutate() cumsum(), first(), lag(). Group-native — fitting functions respect dplyr::group_by() return one row per group, optional parallel fitting via workers.

title: Compute cumulative infiltration from BeerKan pour-event data — beerkan_cumulative
text: Converts raw BeerKan experiment data — fixed volume poured repeatedly time pour infiltrate recorded — cumulative (t) pairs suitable fit_infiltration() fit_best().

title: Compute cumulative infiltration from BeerKan pour-event data — beerkan_cumulative
code: beerkan_cumulative(data, volume_col, time_col, radius)

title: Compute cumulative infiltration from BeerKan pour-event data — beerkan_cumulative
text: data data frame tibble. Passed first argument function compatible |> %>% pipes. volume_col Bare column name; volume poured event (mL). volume may vary pours (e.g. 100 mL , 50 mL last). time_col Bare column name; time pour completely infiltrate (seconds). radius Ring radius cm. default provided ring size varies; always pass actual radius avoid silent errors.

title: Compute cumulative infiltration from BeerKan pour-event data — beerkan_cumulative
text: input data tibble four additional columns: .cumulative_time — cumulative infiltration time (s) .sqrt_time — square root .cumulative_time (s^0.5); provided compatibility fit_infiltration() .cumulative_volume — cumulative volume infiltrated (mL) .infiltration — cumulative infiltration depth (cm)

title: Compute cumulative infiltration from BeerKan pour-event data — beerkan_cumulative
text: BeerKan protocol records data inverted direction compared standard ring Minidisk experiments: Standard / Minidisk: volume measured fixed time steps BeerKan: time measured fixed-volume pour disappear Cumulative quantities computed : $$t_i = \sum_{j=1}^{} \Delta t_j$$ $$I_i = \frac{\sum_{j=1}^{} V_j}{\pi r^2}$$ function group-aware: data grouped dplyr::group_by(), cumulative sums computed independently within group (using dplyr::mutate() cumsum()).

title: Fit the BEST algorithm to BeerKan cumulative infiltration data — fit_best
text: data data frame tibble, optionally grouped dplyr::group_by(). infiltration_col Bare column name cumulative infiltration depth (cm). Typically .infiltration beerkan_cumulative(). time_col Bare column name cumulative infiltration time (s). Typically .cumulative_time beerkan_cumulative(). theta_s Saturated volumetric water content (m³/m³ cm³/cm³). Bare column name scalar. theta_i Initial volumetric water content (m³/m³ cm³/cm³). Bare column name scalar. Must < theta_s. n Van Genuchten shape parameter (dimensionless, must > 1). Bare column name scalar. Typically obtained infiltration_vg_params() supplied directly particle-size analysis. method Fitting method: "steady" (default), "slope", "intercept". See BEST algorithm section . beta Haverkamp β integral shape parameter (default 0.6). Standard value Haverkamp et al. (1994); override calibrated values. gamma Haverkamp γ proportionality constant 3D lateral flow correction (default 0.75). used current 1D implementation retained future 3D extension. steady_n Number terminal data points used define steady-state window method = "steady" method = "intercept". title: Fit the BEST algorithm to BeerKan cumulative infiltration data — fit_best
text: θᵣ assumed zero Sᵢ calculation (θᵢ / θₛ), standard BEST simplification. θᵣ estimate, pass theta_i = theta_i - theta_r adjusted initial saturation.

title: Fit the BEST algorithm to BeerKan cumulative infiltration data — fit_best
text: BEST uses two-term approximation Haverkamp et al. (1994) quasi-exact implicit (QEI) infiltration model. late time (steady state) cumulative curve linear: $$(t) \approx K_s t + b$$ intercept \(b\) encodes sorptivity: $$b = \frac{S^2}{2 K_s \beta (1 - S_i)}, \quad S_i = \theta_i / \theta_s$$ Three fitting methods available: "steady" (default, recommended): Detects steady-state portion last steady_n points (default 4; following R-algorithm Haverkamp et al.). Linear regression subset gives Ks (slope) b (intercept), S back-calculated. "slope": Estimates S early-time transient (slope vs √t points last steady_n), Ks late-time intercept b. "intercept": Linear regression full steady-state subset slope (→ Ks) intercept (→ b → S), identical "steady" uses earliest possible steady-state onset (first point rate change falls rate_tol). estimating Ks S, Van Genuchten α recovered: $$\alpha = \frac{2 (\theta_s - \theta_i) K_s}{c_p \cdot S^2}$$ \(c_p(n) = \bigl[(2m+1) \cdot B(m+\tfrac{1}{2},\tfrac{1}{2})\bigr]^{-1}\) \(m = 1 - 1/n\). evaluated via base R's beta() function — external dependencies, fully vectorised.

title: Fit the BEST algorithm to BeerKan cumulative infiltration data — fit_best
text: Lassabatère, L., Angulo-Jaramillo, R., Soria Ugalde, J. M., Cuenca, R., Braud, ., & Haverkamp, R. (2006). Beerkan estimation soil transfer parameters infiltration experiments—BEST. Soil Science Society America Journal, 70(2), 521–532. https://doi.org/10.2136/sssaj2005.0026 Haverkamp, R., Ross, P. J., Smettem, K. R. J., & Parlange, J.-Y. (1994). Three-dimensional analysis infiltration disc infiltrometer: 2. Physically based infiltration equation. Water Resources Research, 30(11), 2931–2935.

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration
text: data data frame tibble, optionally grouped dplyr::group_by(). infiltration_col Bare column name cumulative infiltration depth (cm). Typically .infiltration infiltration_cumulative() beerkan_cumulative(). sqrt_time_col Bare column name square root elapsed time (s^0.5). Typically .sqrt_time. workers Number parallel workers. Defaults 1 (sequential). Values > 1 use parallel::mclapply() Unix; silently reduced 1 Windows.

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration
text: tibble one row per group (one row ungrouped data) containing: Group keys (input grouped) .C1 — quadratic coefficient (cm/s), time-proportional term .C2 — linear coefficient (cm/s^0.5), sorptivity term .C1_std_error, .C2_std_error — standard errors .convergence — TRUE lm() succeeded

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration
text: fit uses ordinary least squares linearised form ~ sqrt_time + (sqrt_time^2) (intercept), matching published methodology (infiltrodiscR, Zhang 1997 tables). Interpretation mode: Minidisk: C₁ feeds hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk() via K(h) = C₁ / . Ring (ponded): C₁ ≈ Ksat steady state. BeerKan: C₁ provides quick Philip estimate running full fit_best() algorithm. data grouped dplyr::group_by(), model fitted independently group, returning one row per group result. Parallel fitting across groups available Unix-like systems via workers > 1.

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration
text: Philip, J. R. (1957). theory infiltration: 4. Sorptivity algebraic infiltration equations. Soil Science, 84(3), 257–264. Zhang, R. (1997). Determination soil sorptivity hydraulic conductivity disk infiltrometer. Soil Science Society America Journal, 61(4), 1024–1030. https://doi.org/10.2136/sssaj1997.03615995006100060008x

title: Fit the Philip two-term infiltration model — fit_infiltration
code: library(tibble) library(dplyr)  dat <- tibble(   time   = seq(0, 300, 30),   volume = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67) ) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume)  fit_infiltration(dat,                  infiltration_col = .infiltration,                  sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>      .C2     .C1 .C2_std_error .C1_std_error .convergence #>    <dbl>   <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 0.0600 0.00252       0.00753      0.000396 TRUE          # Grouped: one row per sample multi <- tibble(   sample = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 11),   time   = rep(seq(0, 300, 30), 2),   volume = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67,              83, 77, 64, 61, 58, 45, 42, 35, 29, 17, 15) ) |>   group_by(sample) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume)  fit_infiltration(multi,                  infiltration_col = .infiltration,                  sqrt_time_col    = .sqrt_time) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   sample    .C2     .C1 .C2_std_error .C1_std_error .convergence #>   <chr>   <dbl>   <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 A      0.0600 0.00252       0.00753      0.000396 TRUE         #> 2 B      0.0160 0.0135        0.0363       0.00191  TRUE

Inputs typically interval rates computed infiltration_rate(): .rate (y) .time_mid (x). first row within group NA values automatically excluded fitting. title: Fit the Kostiakov (1932) cumulative infiltration model — fit_infiltration_kostiakov
text: model linearised \(\log = \log + b \log t\) derive starting values, refined nls() original scale. instantaneous infiltration rate corresponding model \(f(t) = \cdot b \cdot t^{b-1}\), decreases monotonically time \(0 < b < 1\). Note Kostiakov model approach finite steady-state value (\(f \0\) \(t \\infty\)), used estimate Ksat. first row within group typically t = 0, produce zero undefined logarithm. Rows time == 0 infiltration == 0 automatically excluded fitting. data grouped, model fitted independently per group.

title: Look up Van Genuchten parameters from the Minidisk reference table — infiltration_vg_params
code: library(tibble)  soils <- tibble(   site    = c("A", "B"),   texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   suction = c(4, 2) ) infiltration_vg_params(soils, texture = texture, suction = suction) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   site  texture    suction    .n .alpha    .A #>   <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A     sandy loam       4  1.89  0.075  3.95 #> 2 B     clay loam        2  1.31  0.019  6.64  # Scalar suction infiltration_vg_params(soils, texture = texture, suction = 3) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   site  texture    suction    .n .alpha    .A #>   <chr> <chr>        <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A     sandy loam       4  1.89  0.075  3.93 #> 2 B     clay loam        2  1.31  0.019  7.23  # Character suction string soils2 <- tibble(texture = "loam", suction = "2cm") infiltration_vg_params(soils2, texture = texture, suction = suction) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   texture suction    .n .alpha    .A #>   <chr>   <chr>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 loam    2cm      1.56  0.036  6.27  # For BeerKan: retrieve shape params only (A is NA at suction = 0) beerkan_meta <- tibble(texture = "sandy loam", theta_s = 0.45, theta_i = 0.1) infiltration_vg_params(beerkan_meta, texture = texture, suction = 0) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   texture    theta_s theta_i    .n .alpha    .A #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 sandy loam    0.45     0.1  1.89  0.075    NA

title: Compute unsaturated hydraulic conductivity from Minidisk data — minidisk_conductivity
text: data data frame tibble, typically output fit_infiltration() joined sample metadata. texture Bare column name character scalar; USDA texture class (e.g. "Sandy Loam"). Must match names minidisk_vg_params. suction Bare column name numeric scalar; applied suction cm (e.g. 2). Must one tabulated levels (0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7) method = "tabulated". C1 Bare column name; Philip \(C_1\) coefficient (cm/s). Defaults .C1 (column produced fit_infiltration()). method "tabulated" (default) uses Decagon lookup table; "zhang" computes analytically Zhang (1997). radius Disc radius cm. used method = "zhang". Defaults 2.25.

title: Compute unsaturated hydraulic conductivity from Minidisk data — minidisk_conductivity
text: input tibble .n, .alpha, ., .K_h appended. method = "zhang" .column reflects analytically computed value rather tabulated one.

title: Compute unsaturated hydraulic conductivity from Minidisk data — minidisk_conductivity
text: Two methods available -parameter: "tabulated" Looks Decagon Devices (2005) reference table via infiltration_vg_params(). Valid 8 standard Minidisk suction levels (0.5–7 cm) 12 USDA texture classes. table assumes disc radius 2.25 cm. "zhang" Computes analytically Zhang (1997) via parameter_A_zhang(). Valid suction disc radius; uses \(n\) \(\alpha\) values VG lookup inputs.

title: Compute unsaturated hydraulic conductivity from Minidisk data — minidisk_conductivity
code: library(tibble) library(dplyr)  # Minimal single-sample pipeline: raw data -> K(h) in four steps minidisk <- tibble(   time   = seq(0, 300, 30),   volume = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67),   texture = "Sandy Loam",   suction = 2 )  minidisk |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = texture, suction = suction) #> Error: object 'texture' not found  # Multi-sample pipeline using group_by — no double group_by needed multi <- tibble(   sample  = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 11),   time    = rep(seq(0, 300, 30), 2),   volume  = c(95, 89, 86, 83, 80, 77, 74, 73, 71, 69, 67,               83, 77, 74, 71, 68, 65, 62, 59, 57, 55, 53),   texture = rep(c("Sandy Loam", "Loam"), each = 11),   suction = 2 )  multi |>   group_by(sample) |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = texture, suction = suction) #> Error: object 'texture' not found  # Analytical A via Zhang (1997) minidisk |>   infiltration_cumulative(time = time, volume = volume) |>   fit_infiltration(.infiltration, .sqrt_time) |>   minidisk_conductivity(texture = texture, suction = suction,                         method = "zhang", radius = 2.25) #> Error: object 'texture' not found

title: Compute the Zhang (1997) A parameter for Minidisk analysis — parameter_A_zhang
text: data data frame tibble. n Van Genuchten n parameter (dimensionless, must > 1). Bare column name scalar. alpha Van Genuchten α parameter (1/cm, must > 0). Bare column name scalar. suction Applied suction cm (absolute value used internally). Bare column name scalar. radius Disc radius cm. Defaults 2.25 (Minidisk standard).

title: Compute the Zhang (1997) A parameter for Minidisk analysis — parameter_A_zhang
text: input data tibble one additional column ..

title: Compute the Zhang (1997) A parameter for Minidisk analysis — parameter_A_zhang
text: $$= \frac{11.65 \cdot (n^{0.1} - 1) \cdot   \exp\!\bigl(B(n) \cdot (n - 1.9) \cdot \alpha \cdot |h|\bigr)}   {(\alpha \cdot r_0)^{0.91}}$$ \(B(n) = 7.5\) \(n < 1.9\) \(B(n) = 2.92\) otherwise. column arguments accept bare column names scalar numeric values, following tidy evaluation convention TidySoils ecosystem.

title: Compute the Zhang (1997) A parameter for Minidisk analysis — parameter_A_zhang
text: Zhang, R. (1997). Determination soil sorptivity hydraulic conductivity disk infiltrometer. Soil Science Society America Journal, 61(4), 1024–1030. https://doi.org/10.2136/sssaj1997.03615995006100060008x

title: Compute the Zhang (1997) A parameter for Minidisk analysis — parameter_A_zhang
code: library(tibble)  soils <- tibble(   site   = c("A", "B"),   n      = c(1.89, 1.56),   alpha  = c(0.075, 0.036),   suction = c(4, 2) )  # Using bare column names parameter_A_zhang(soils, n = n, alpha = alpha, suction = suction) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site      n alpha suction    .A #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A      1.89 0.075       4  3.78 #> 2 B      1.56 0.036       2  4.34  # Scalar suction, column n and alpha parameter_A_zhang(soils, n = n, alpha = alpha, suction = 3) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site      n alpha suction    .A #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A      1.89 0.075       4  3.80 #> 2 B      1.56 0.036       2  3.96  # Non-standard disc radius parameter_A_zhang(soils, n = n, alpha = alpha, suction = suction, radius = 3) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   site      n alpha suction    .A #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A      1.89 0.075       4  2.91 #> 2 B      1.56 0.036       2  3.34

title: Estimate saturated hydraulic conductivity from ponded ring infiltration — ring_conductivity
text: data data frame tibble, optionally grouped dplyr::group_by(). grouped, model fitted independently per group. time Bare column name; elapsed time seconds. volume Bare column name; water volume reservoir (mL), decreasing water infiltrates. radius Ring radius cm. default — ring sizes vary silent wrong value produce incorrect Kfs estimates. workers Number parallel workers passed fit_infiltration_horton(). Defaults 1. Values > 1 use parallel::mclapply() (Unix ).

title: Estimate saturated hydraulic conductivity from ponded ring infiltration — ring_conductivity
text: tibble one row per group containing: Group keys (grouped) .fc — final/steady-state infiltration rate ≈ Kfs (cm/s) .f0 — initial infiltration rate (cm/s) .k — exponential decay constant (1/s) .fc_std_error, .f0_std_error, .k_std_error .convergence — logical; TRUE NLS converged

title: Estimate saturated hydraulic conductivity from ponded ring infiltration — ring_conductivity
text: Horton model : $$f(t) = f_c + (f_0 - f_c) \cdot e^{-k t}$$ \(f_c\) ≈ Kfs (cm/s), \(f_0\) initial infiltration rate, \(k\) (1/s) exponential decay constant. use step--step approach instead: want visualise raw cumulative infiltration curve rate decay fitting, call infiltration_cumulative(), infiltration_rate(), fit_infiltration_horton() individually.

title: Estimate saturated hydraulic conductivity from ponded ring infiltration — ring_conductivity
text: Horton, R. E. (1940). approach toward physical interpretation infiltration capacity. Soil Science Society America Proceedings, 5, 399–417.

title: Estimate saturated hydraulic conductivity from ponded ring infiltration — ring_conductivity
code: library(tibble) library(dplyr)  # Single ring — 100 cm ring radius, 1-hour run ring <- tibble(   time   = seq(0, 3600, 360),   volume = c(500, 440, 390, 355, 328, 308, 292, 280, 271, 265, 260) )  ring_conductivity(ring, time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>        .fc     .f0      .k .fc_std_error .f0_std_error .k_std_error .convergence #>      <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 -1.46e-5 6.18e-4 7.14e-4     0.0000187     0.0000156    0.0000611 TRUE          # Multi-site: one group_by feeds the full pipeline multi <- tibble(   site   = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 11),   time   = rep(seq(0, 3600, 360), 2),   volume = c(     500, 440, 390, 355, 328, 308, 292, 280, 271, 265, 260,     500, 450, 410, 375, 348, 328, 313, 302, 294, 288, 284   ) )  multi |>   group_by(site) |>   ring_conductivity(time = time, volume = volume, radius = 10) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   site         .fc      .f0       .k .fc_std_error .f0_std_error .k_std_error #>   <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 A     -0.0000146 0.000618 0.000714     0.0000187     0.0000156    0.0000611 #> 2 B     -0.0000846 0.000492 0.000476     0.0000309     0.0000114    0.0000547 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: .convergence <lgl>

title: tidysoilinfiltration 1.1.0
text: minidisk_conductivity() — convenience wrapper combining infiltration_vg_params(), optionally parameter_A_zhang(), hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk() single pipeline step. Accepts method = "tabulated" (default, Decagon lookup) method = "zhang" (analytical via Zhang 1997). Reduces Minidisk pipeline 7 steps 4 eliminates double group_by(). ring_conductivity() — convenience wrapper combining infiltration_cumulative(), infiltration_rate(), fit_infiltration_horton() one call. Takes raw time–volume readings ponded ring infiltrometer returns Horton parameters including .fc ≈ Kfs. Supports group_by() workflow package functions.

title: tidysoilinfiltration 1.1.0
text: infiltration_cumulative() infiltration_rate() previously stripped group_by() grouping via internal as_tibble() call, requiring users re-group calling fit_infiltration(). functions now preserve grouping pipeline, single group_by() sufficient entire multi-site workflow.

title: tidysoilinfiltration 1.0.0
text: Initial release.

title: tidysoilinfiltration 1.0.0
text: infiltration_cumulative() — convert time–volume readings Minidisk ring infiltrometers cumulative infiltration depth (t) √t. Fully group-aware. beerkan_cumulative() — convert BeerKan pour-event data (fixed volumes, infiltration times) cumulative (t). Group-aware via cumsum(). infiltration_rate() — compute mean interval infiltration rates midpoint times cumulative data. Group-aware. fit_infiltration() — fit Philip (1957) two-term polynomial = C₂√t + C₁t OLS. Returns C₁ (conductivity proxy) C₂ (sorptivity proxy). Supports parallel fitting across groups. infiltration_vg_params() — look Van Genuchten n, α, Decagon Devices (2005) table texture class suction. Pass suction = 0 BeerKan (shape parameters ; .NA). parameter_A_zhang() — compute Zhang (1997) parameter analytically n, α, suction, disc radius. Fully vectorised. hydraulic_conductivity_minidisk() — compute K(h) = C₁ / . fit_infiltration_horton() — fit Horton (1940) exponential rate decay model NLS. Returns fc ≈ Ksat, f₀, k. fit_infiltration_kostiakov() — fit Kostiakov (1932) power model = t^b NLS. Returns , b. fit_best() — implement BEST algorithm (Lassabatère et al., 2006). Estimates Ks, S, α BeerKan cumulative data using Haverkamp et al. (1994) quasi-exact implicit model. Three fitting methods: "steady" (default), "slope", "intercept". Supports parallel fitting.
